data.title: Fed meeting today expected to announce more hawkish near-term interest-rate outlook. #FedMeeting #HawkishShift
data.description: Today's meeting of the Federal Reserve is expected to indicate a more hawkish near-term interest-rate projection. It is anticipated that there will be a shift towards a more aggressive stance in terms of interest rates. This new development follows concerns raised post the Fed meeting.

data.title: Iran's position weakens post Hamas attack on Israel Oct 7, 2023; stocks hit. Iran in crisis.
data.description: Iran's strategic standing deteriorated following the attack by Hamas on Israel on October 7, 2023. The incident has led to a significant decline in Iran's influence. Meanwhile, stock prices have been impacted as Israel comes under fire. The attack marks a turning point in the region's dynamics and has raised concerns about stability. The event underscores the volatile nature of the situation and the uncertain future that lies ahead.

data.title: U.S.-China trade deal: 55% tariff on Chinese goods, rare earth dispute settled. More details to come.
data.description: President Trump announced that the U.S.-China trade agreement involves raising tariffs on Chinese products to 55%, with the inclusion of rare earths in the deal. In return, China is expected to receive undisclosed benefits.

data.title: U.S.-China trade talks may involve U.S. easing chip restrictions in exchange for access to rare earths due to Beijing's export curbs.
data.description: Negotiations between the United States and China regarding trade have become more pressing due to Beijing's restrictions on the export of rare earth minerals. Analysts from Wall Street suggest that the U.S. may consider relaxing restrictions on semiconductor chips as a bargaining chip in these talks. This development is significant as it could potentially impact the outcome of the trade discussions between the two countries.

data.title: Jobs report shows decent hiring but labor market cooling with plunge in employment and labor force participation. S&P 500 rises.
data.description: The recent household survey revealed a significant decrease in both employment rates and labor force participation. Despite this, the latest Jobs Report indicates a decent level of hiring, suggesting a subtle cooling of the labor market. Additionally, the S&P 500 has experienced a rise following these updates.

data.title: Trump criticizes Fed after weak ADP jobs report; private hiring weakest since March 2023. S&P 500 increases.
data.description: Private hiring experienced its lowest growth since March 2023. Following a disappointing ADP jobs report, former president Trump criticized the Fed. Despite this, the S&P 500 saw an increase.

data.title: U.S.-China trade talks in jeopardy; tech war escalates; S&P 500 futures drop. tensions escalate - watch for updates.
data.description: The trade truce between U.S. and China is at risk due to allegations of deal violations from both sides. The escalation of the tech war adds pressure. Keep an eye on developments as S&P 500 futures drop in response to the uncertain situation.

data.title: Key Fed inflation rate drops to 4-year low, lowest since March 2021, causing S&P 500 to dip.
data.description: The Federal Reserve's key inflation rate dropped to its lowest point since March 2021. This marks a 4-year low for the rate. As a result, the S&P 500 experienced a dip in response.

data.title: US-China trade deal cuts tariffs by over 100%, boosts S&P 500. Risks still loom.
data.description: The recent trade agreement between the United States and China resulted in a significant reduction in tariffs, with rates dropping by more than 100%. This move caused a surge in the S&P 500 index on Monday, reflecting a positive response from investors. However, despite the rally in the market, uncertainties and potential risks still loom ahead. This deal marks a significant development in the trade relations between the two nations, driving optimism among market participants.

data.title: US-UK trade deal maintains 10% tariff; S&P 500 rallies. Amazon, Google, Meta set to benefit.
data.description: On Thursday, a trade deal between the United States and the United Kingdom was unveiled, marking the initial of a series aimed at mitigating tariffs imposed during the Trump administration. Companies such as Amazon, Google, and Meta stand to gain from this agreement. Despite maintaining a 10% tariff, the deal has sparked a surge in the S&P 500. This development is anticipated to set the stage for similar trade pacts in the future.

data.title: Jewel Thais-Williams, founder of Catch One nightclub in LA in 1973, known for celebrating 'Queer Black Joy,' dies at 86.
data.description: Established in 1973 in Los Angeles despite encountering resistance, Catch One transformed into a beloved haven for an ostracized community.

data.title: Cal-Maine Foods, Inc. publishes Fiscal 2024 Sustainability Report, emphasizing progress on key initiatives such as UN SDGs.
data.description: In Ridgeland, Mississippi, Cal-Maine Foods, Inc. (NASDAQ: CALM) has unveiled its latest Fiscal 2024 Sustainability Report. The report showcases the company's advancements in key sustainability projects. Cal-Maine Foods continues to align its data with specific aspects of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals and the Sustainability Accounting Standards Board (SASB) guidelines. Sherman Miller, the company's President and CEO, presented the update.

data.title: One Step GPS wins 2025 Grand Stevie Award for customer service excellence, highest-rated nomination of the year.
data.description: In San Fernando, California, on July 18, 2025, One Step GPS proudly revealed that it has received the prestigious 2025 Grand Stevie Award for the highest-rated nomination of the year for customer service. The company attributes this remarkable achievement to its team's dedicated efforts and unwavering commitment.

data.title: Stocks stagnant, Dow drops due to Trump-EU tariff threat; Netflix plunges; Fed official suggests July rate cut.
data.description: Today, the stock market experienced relatively little activity in major stock indexes, although there were significant movements in several leading stocks. On Friday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average declined following reports of potential tariffs between the US and EU, while Netflix saw a substantial decline. Additionally, a Federal Reserve official advocated for a rate cut in July. This information was covered in live updates by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Single female home buyers face challenges with student loan debt while purchasing homes; details about their financial realities.
data.description: A recent report delves into the financial challenges encountered by single women purchasing homes, highlighting varying approaches and obstacles faced within this demographic. Authored by Amethyst Marroquin, the study explores how single female home buyers navigate the real estate market. Particularly focusing on their interactions with different buyer demographics and the impact of student loan debt on their purchasing power. The report sheds light on the financial realities experienced by single women in the housing market, indicating the complexities they encounter in the home-buying process.

data.title: Republicans are close to a major victory in reducing the powers of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, a years-long effort.
data.description: Republicans are getting close to achieving their most significant victory in their ongoing efforts to weaken the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau.

data.title: 30-year fixed mortgage rate remains unchanged at 6.49% on August 15, 2024, compared to 6.47% last week - Freddie Mac report.
data.description: Latest Mortgage Rate Update: As of August 15, 2024, the 30-year fixed mortgage rate offered by Freddie Mac stayed stable at 6.49%, a minor increase from the previous week's rate of 6.47%. This information was provided by Jessica Lautz, an expert in Mortgages & Home Financing. These figures indicate a consistent trend in mortgage rates over the past few weeks.

data.title: Wall Street rises as trade uncertainty eases, new tariffs delayed to Aug 1. Nvidia's market cap hits $4 trillion as it plans new AI chip for China.
data.description: Stocks on Wall Street saw an increase on Wednesday as investors brushed off trade uncertainties following the White House’s decision to delay new tariffs until August 1, raising optimism for further negotiations. President Donald Trump had mentioned imposing significant tariffs, such as a 50% tariff on copper and a potential 200% tariff on pharmaceuticals, with a possible grace period for the latter. The Russell 2000 saw the most growth, climbing 0.7% to reach 2,244, showcasing a resurgence in small-cap stocks. The Nasdaq 100 also experienced a 0.5% rise to 22,815, driven by strong performance from large-cap tech companies. Nvidia Corp. made headlines once more as its market capitalization surpassed $4 trillion, solidifying its position as the world’s most valuable company. Reports suggest Nvidia is gearing to introduce a new AI chip tailored for the Chinese market by September.

data.title: Nvidia reaches $4 trillion market cap, driving Wall Street to record highs. Shares double in 3 months. Magnificent Seven tech companies see combined market value rise.
data.description: This week, Wall Street reached new record levels with Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ: NVDA) leading the charge as the first company to exceed a $4 trillion market value. The tech and AI sectors were invigorated by this milestone. Nvidia's stock has surged for seven consecutive weeks and 11 out of the last 12, with its share price nearly doubling from early April lows in just over three months. The combined market capitalization of the top seven companies, including Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL), Microsoft Corp. (NASDAQ: MSFT), Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN), Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ: META), Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ: GOOGL), Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA), and Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ: NVDA), also rose significantly. For more details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: In 2024, new REALTORS are more diverse than experienced counterparts, as per the NAR Member Profile.
data.description: In the 2024 NAR Member Profile, it was found that new REALTORS® stand out for their diversity compared to the experienced members. This indicates a shift towards more inclusivity in the industry. The data shows that a significant number of new NAR members come from various backgrounds, highlighting a positive trend towards greater representation. This highlights a noteworthy contrast between the demographics of new and experienced real estate professionals in 2024.
